rulings.Case Title: EDNA CANNON et al VS. CRYSTAL IDELLA CANNON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, line 1, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action., overruled as to the fifth, sixth, and eighth causes of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. As to the first, second, and third causes of action, Plaintiffs do not allege facts to show a fiduciary relationship with Defendant Bank of America and it is unlikely that further leave to amend can cure this defect. As for the fourth cause of action, Plaintiffs fail to allege, with sufficient particularity, any facts to support a cause of action for fraud against Defendant Bank of America and it is unlikely further leave to amend can cure this defect. The fifth, sixth, and eighth causes of action are adequately pled. As to the seventh cause of action, Plaintiffs are given leave to allege, if they can do so in good faith, their performance or excuse for non-performance of the contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHUCK A WONG et al VS. MURAL MEDIA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT MURAL MEDIA, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The moving parties did not provide courtesy copies of their moving papers to Department 302. If defendants wish to have their motion heard, they shall provide courtesy copies of their moving papers to Department 302 on Monday, July 9, 2012 at 4:00 pm. The hearing of this motion is continued to July 16, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. Since Plaintiffs failed to file any papers in opposition to the motion, they may not use this additional week to file opposition papers, and this motion will be deemed unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AS)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BRADLEY VS. HWA 555 OWNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Hwa 55 Owneers Llc, And Vornado Realty Trust'S Motion For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence (Part 3)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, Line 2. Defendants Hwa 55 Owneers Llc, And Vornado Realty Trust'S Motion For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence (Part 3). The Court further notes that plaintiff's expert submitted a declaration explaining that the type of testing he performed does not require analysis of the actual footwear worn but, rather, uses a standard test shoe material. Defendants have submitted nothing to question or refute this evidence. This case is distinguishable from Puritan Insurance Co. v. Superior Court, 171 Cal. App. 3d 877 (1985), in which the court imposed a limited evidentiary sanction where one party's expert had used evidence in his testing that was later lost and unavailable to the other party's expert. In this case, there was no similar unfairness, as plaintiff's expert did not have access to the lost boot and did not use it in his testing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to TSchoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AS)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BRADLEY VS. HWA 555 OWNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Hwa 55 Owneers Llc, And Vornado Realty Trust'S Motion For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence (Part 2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, Line 2. Defendants Hwa 55 Owneers Llc, And Vornado Realty Trust'S Motion For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence (Part 2). As to defendants' request for issue and evidentiary sanctions under the Discovery Act, absent a prior court order compelling discovery, such sanctions are only available in "exceptional circumstances" to remedy "misconduct" that is "sufficiently egregious." See New Albertsons, Inc. v. Superior Court, 168 Cal. App. 4th 1403, 1426, 1428 (2009). Here, there is no prior court order, thus, issue and evidentiary sanctions are not proper absent such "exceptional circumstances." The record here lacks any evidence of "exceptional circumstances," which typically involve some variety of willful or intentional misconduct. See id. at 1428-1430 (discussing "exceptional circumstances") The evidence presently before the Court appears to be straightforward and largely, if not entirely, undisputed. Specifically, plaintiff's left boot was removed at the time of her treatment following the slip-and-fall incident in March 2010 and was not returned to her; plaintiff discarded her right boot sometime around May or June 2010; and at the time she discarded the right boot, she had not yet met with an attorney or filed a lawsuit. The record here does not reveal the type of "sufficiently egregious" conduct that would give rise to sanctions under the Discovery Act. While Defendants ask the Court to infer that plaintiff "immediately appreciated the potential for litigation," the Court does not find support for this conclusion in the record. Similarly, the record does not support a conclusion that the right boot was discarded "in anticipation of a discovery request." Cedars-Sinai Medical Center v. Superior Court, 18 Cal. 4th 1, 12 (1998). = (302/AS)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BRADLEY VS. HWA 555 OWNERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Teminating, Issue, And Evidentiary Sanctions Based On Spoliation Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANTS HWA 555 OWNERS LLC AND VORNADO REALTY TRUST'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Teminating, Issue, And Evidentiary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. As to terminating sanctions, the record before the Court does not come close to supporting such an extreme sanction. The cases on which defendants rely only allow terminating sanctions in egregious cases of intentional destruction of evidence. See R.S. Creative, Inc. v. Creative Cotton, Ltd., 75 Cal. App. 4th 486, 496 (1999) ("[T]erminating sanctions are to be used sparingly, only when the trial court concludes that lesser sanctions would not bring about the compliance of the offending party. The record demonstrates repeated violations of stipulations and court orders, a forged document offered as true, and deliberate destruction of evidence pertinent to exposing that fact."); Williams v. Russ, 167 Cal. App. 4th 1215, 1223 (2009) ("A terminating sanction is appropriate in the first instance without a violation of prior court orders in egregious cases of intentional spoliation of evidence.") Nothing remotely similar appears in the record here. Accordingly, terminating sanctions are not proper. = (302/AS)

rulings.Case Title: SFS 39, INC. VS. JENNIFER WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memo Of P & A; Appendix Of Foreign Authorities And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, line 7, PETITIONERs SFS 39, INC., ANDREA FRONCILLO, JERRY DAL BOZZO, DANTE SERAFINI's motion To Vacate Arbitration Award. Granted. The arbitrator exceeded his authority in ordering the class claims to arbitration because there is not a sufficient basis to conclude that the parties agreed to authorize class arbitration in their arbitration agreements. (Kinecta Alternative Financial Solutions, Inc. v. Superior Court, (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 506.) rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE E. LOPEZ VS. VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 9, 2012, line 17, DEFENDANTS VCA ANTECH, VCA SAN FRANCISCO VETERINARY SPECIALISTS, LP, ALAN STEWART'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend as to the emotional distress and pain and suffering allegations and with twenty days leave to amend as to the punitive damages and attorney's fees allegations. Plaintiff's allegations regarding emotional distress and pain and suffering fail because she does not allege any facts showing that she experienced a physical injury nor is it likely that this defect can be cured by an amendment. Plaintiff has leave to allege: (1) ultimate facts supporting punitive damages per Civil Code 3294 and 3340 and (2) a common law, statutory, or contractual basis for fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
